query:
INSERT INTO `program_lessons` (programId, day, title, concept, exercisePrompt, guidanceTemplate, `order`) VALUES
(30002, 15, 'Building the New Narrator', 'You''ve spent two weeks understanding and challenging the old voice. Now we begin the most important work: building a new one. Not a toxic positivity machine that denies reality — a grounded, honest, compassionate narrator who tells the truth and believes in your capacity to handle it.', 'Describe the inner voice you want to have. What does it sound like? How does it respond when you fail? What does it say when you''re afraid? Write a full description — as if you''re casting a character for the most important role in your life.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "The inner voice I''m building sounds like... and the first thing it says to me when I wake up is..." | Micro-Habit: Write one sentence in the voice of your new narrator. Just one. Put it somewhere you''ll see it tomorrow morning.', 15),
(30002, 16, 'The Permission Slip', 'The old voice withheld permission — to rest, to fail, to be seen, to want things, to take up space. Today you write your own permission slip. No one else gets to sign it.', 'What have you been waiting for permission to do, feel, or become? Write a formal permission slip to yourself. Be specific. Be generous. Sign it. Date it. Read it out loud.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "I''ve been waiting for permission to... and today I''m giving it to myself because..." | Micro-Habit: Do one small thing today that the old voice would have talked you out of. It doesn''t have to be big. It just has to be yours.', 16),
(30002, 17, 'Failure as Data', 'The inner critic treats failure as evidence of unworthiness. The new narrator treats failure as data. Same event, completely different relationship to it. Today we practice the reframe that changes everything.', 'Think of a failure you''ve been carrying shame about. Write it out. Then write: "What this failure taught me was... What it does NOT mean about who I am is... What I would do differently is..." Separate the lesson from the identity attack.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "A failure I''ve been carrying too much shame about is... and what it actually taught me was..." | Micro-Habit: Find one thing you failed at this week. Write one sentence about what it taught you. That''s the only sentence that matters.', 17),
(30002, 18, 'The Gratitude Reframe', 'Gratitude isn''t toxic positivity. It''s a neurological intervention. When you genuinely focus on what''s working, the brain''s threat-detection system downregulates. The inner critic loses its primary fuel source. Today we use it strategically.', 'Write about three things in your life that exist because of who you are — not luck, not circumstance, but because of your specific qualities, choices, and effort. Own them fully. Don''t minimize. This isn''t bragging. It''s accurate accounting.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "Three things in my life that exist because of who I actually am — not luck — are..." | Micro-Habit: Before you go to sleep tonight, name three things that happened today that you handled well. Not perfectly. Well.', 18),
(30002, 19, 'The Conversation You''ve Been Avoiding', 'The inner voice often gets loudest around one specific area of your life — a relationship, a career, a dream, a truth you haven''t spoken yet. That loudness is a signal. It''s pointing at something that matters. Today we walk toward it.', 'What is the conversation, decision, or truth you''ve been most avoiding? What does your inner critic say will happen if you face it? What does your new narrator say? Write both voices out. Then ask: which one is actually trying to help you grow?', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "The thing I''ve been most avoiding in my life right now is... and what I think would actually happen if I faced it is..." | Micro-Habit: Take one small step toward the thing you''ve been avoiding. One email. One sentence. One honest moment. Just one.', 19),
(30002, 20, 'Who You''re Becoming', 'Identity change doesn''t happen in a single moment. It happens in the accumulation of small decisions to act like the person you want to be — before you fully believe you are them. Today we cast the vision clearly.', 'Describe who you are becoming. Not who you want to be someday — who you are in the process of becoming right now, based on the choices you''ve made over these 20 days. What is different about you? What has shifted? What are you no longer willing to accept from yourself?', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "The person I am becoming is someone who... and the evidence I have for that is..." | Micro-Habit: Do one thing today that the person you''re becoming would do. Not the old you. The one you just described.', 20),
(30002, 21, 'The Voice You Choose', 'Twenty-one days ago, you had a voice you didn''t choose. Today, you have something you didn''t have before: awareness, evidence, and a practice. The old voice will return. It always does. But now you know what it is, where it came from, and — most importantly — that you are not it. You are the one who hears it. And you get to choose what you do next.', 'Write your final entry: a letter from the person you are today to the person you were on Day 1. What do you want them to know? What has changed? What are you carrying forward? What are you leaving behind? End with the one sentence that, if you remembered nothing else from these 21 days, would be enough.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "Twenty-one days ago I started this because... and what I want to tell that version of myself now is..." | Micro-Habit: Read your Day 1 journal entry. Then read today''s. You are not the same person. That matters more than you know.', 21);
command:
